quranMenu.title: Quran instances
quranMenu.hatimDesc: Opens your part directly from the home page (saves separately)
quranMenu.kahfHadith: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported: The Prophet ﷺ said: "Whoever recites Surah al-Kahf on Friday will have a light between this Friday and the next."

levelDisplayDescriptions.1: First 7 letters of the Arabic alphabet (Alif, Bā, Tā, Thā, Jīm, Ḥā, Khā) in all 4 forms (initial, medial, final, isolated).
levelDisplayDescriptions.2: Second 8 letters: Dāl, Dhāl, Rā, Zāy (2 forms each), Sīn, Shīn, Ṣād, Ḍād (initial, medial, final, isolated).
levelDisplayDescriptions.3: Third 7 letters: Ṭā, Ẓā, ʿAyn, Ghayn, Fā, Qāf, Kāf – 4 forms each (isolated, initial, medial, final).
levelDisplayDescriptions.4: Fourth 6 letters: Lām, Mīm, Nūn, Hā (4 forms each), Wāw (2 forms), Yā (4 forms).
levelDisplayDescriptions.5: 10× single letters with tashkīl, 20× short words (Fatha/Damma/Kasra/Sukūn ۡ) – no Madd, no Shaddah.
levelDisplayDescriptions.6: Recognise and correctly transliterate words with Shaddah (ّ). No Madd.
levelDisplayDescriptions.7: Transliteration given → choose the correct word in Arabic. Write Tanwīn fatḥah (an) with Alif at the end.
levelDisplayDescriptions.8: Read a Quran passage and mark natural Madd. Only natural Madd; no verses with „الله“.
levelDisplayDescriptions.9: Hamza (ء، أ، إ، ئ، ؤ) and Tāʾ marbūṭa (ة): recognise and transliterate. No Tanwīn, no Madd.
levelDisplayDescriptions.10: Recognise and correctly transliterate words with آ، لا/لأ، ىٰ.
levelDisplayDescriptions.12: Pause and stop signs in the Quran.
levelDisplayDescriptions.13: Distinguish and read Madd Muttassil (connected) and Madd Mufassil (separate).
levelDisplayDescriptions.14: Recognise and read connecting Madd and Madd Lazim.
levelDisplayDescriptions.15: Distinguish Hamzat al-Waṣl and Hamzat al-Qaṭʿ and pronounce correctly.
levelDisplayDescriptions.16: Read the word "Allah" with correct Tafkhīm/Tarqīq.
levelDisplayDescriptions.17: Nūn Sakinah and Tanwīn: Iẓhār, Idghām, Iqlāb, Ikhfāʾ.
levelDisplayDescriptions.18: Mīm Sakinah: Iẓhār Shafawī, Idghām Shafawī, Ikhfāʾ Shafawī.
levelDisplayDescriptions.19: Rā rules: when thick (Tafkhīm), when thin (Tarqīq).
levelDisplayDescriptions.20: Recognise and read Qalqalah with ق ط ب ج د.

1.title: Pronouncing letters correctly
1.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
1.modalGoalText: You can pronounce each letter clearly and correctly.

modalBullets.0: Where each letter is pronounced (throat, tongue, lips)
modalBullets.1: Differences between similar letters (e.g. س / ص, ت / ط)

2.title: Harakāt – short vowels
2.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
2.modalGoalText: You can read short vowels confidently and fluently.

modalBullets.3: How to connect letters fluently with vowels

3.title: Sukūn – letters without vowel
3.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
3.modalGoalText: You read silent letters cleanly and correctly.

modalBullets.0: That Sukūn (small circle) means a letter stays silent
modalBullets.1: How to read letters with Sukūn correctly, without extra sound

4.title: Shaddah – double letters
4.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
4.modalGoalText: You read words with Shaddah clearly and powerfully, as in the Qur'an.

modalBullets.0: Shaddah (w-shaped sign) means a letter is doubled/strengthened
modalBullets.1: How to hear and pronounce Shaddah correctly
modalBullets.2: How to recognize Shaddah when reading and read fluently
modalBullets.3: Tanwīn (ـٌ ـٍ ـً): Double-vowel signs at word end read as "-un", "-in" or "-an"
modalBullets.4: Tāʾ marbūṭa (ة): Pronounced as "h" when stopping, as "t" when continuing

5.title: Elongation (basic rule)
5.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
5.modalGoalText: You read elongations steadily and correctly.

modalBullets.0: Harakāt are small "count steps" for length – 1 Haraka ≈ normal short vowel length
modalBullets.1: When a sound is lengthened (Alif, Waw, Ya without vowel)
modalBullets.3: Avoid common mistakes: reading too short or too long

6.title: Rules for the N sound
6.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
6.modalGoalText: You can automatically recognize N rules and read them correctly.

modalBullets.0: Iẓhār: N (نْ or Tanwīn) before throat letters (ء ه ع ح غ خ) is pronounced clearly as "n"
modalBullets.1: Idghām: N merges with next letter (ي ن م و ل ر)
modalBullets.2: Iqlāb: N before ب is pronounced as "m"
modalBullets.3: Ikhfāʾ: N before other letters is pronounced nasally/muffled

7.title: Rules for the M sound
7.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn the rules for Mim Sakinah:
7.modalGoalText: You recognize Mim Sakinah positions reliably and read them with the correct rule.

modalBullets.0: Before all letters except ب and م: Mīm pronounced clearly (Iẓhār Shafawī)
modalBullets.1: Before م: Mīm merged with Ghunnah for ~2 Harakāt (Idghām Shafawī)
modalBullets.2: Before ب: Mīm concealed with Ghunnah for ~2 Harakāt (Ikhfāʾ Shafawī)

8.title: The heavy "L" in Allah
8.description: When Allah is pronounced thick or thin
8.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:

modalBullets.0: When ل in "Allah" sounds thick/heavy (after Fatha/Damma)

9.title: Echo letters (Qalqalah)
9.description: Kutb Jadd (q, t, b, j, d)
9.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
9.modalGoalText: You recognize when Qalqalah occurs and read clearly when stopping.

modalBullets.0: Qalqalah: short "echo" sound for ق ط ب ج د (Kutb Jadd)
modalBullets.1: Qalqalah only when these letters have Sukūn (written or at word end when stopping)
modalBullets.2: No Qalqalah when the letter has Fatha/Kasra/Damma and you don't stop
modalBullets.3: The echo is stronger when stopping at word end

10.title: Hamza rules
10.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn:
10.modalGoalText: You read Hamza cleanly, set Hamzat al-Waṣl with a/i/u correctly, and connect words fluently.

modalBullets.0: When Hamza is always read (Hamzat al-Qaṭʿ)
modalBullets.1: When Hamza is only read at start (Hamzat al-Waṣl) and drops when connecting
modalBullets.2: Choosing the right vowel for Hamzat al-Waṣl at start: a / i / u
modalBullets.3: Connecting words fluently without wrong starts

11.title: Advanced elongation
11.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn the rules for Madd Lāzim and Madd Wājib:
11.modalGoalText: You recognize Madd Lāzim and Madd Wājib reliably and can read them with the correct length.

modalBullets.0: Madd Lāzim: Madd letter followed by Shaddah → always 6 Harakāt
modalBullets.1: Madd Wājib: Madd letter followed by Hamzah in same word → 4–5 Harakāt

12.title: R rules
12.description: When Ra is thick or thin
12.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn the rules for Rā:
12.modalGoalText: You recognize when ر is read thin or thick – including the special cases with Madd-Ya.

modalBullets.1: Rā sākin after letter with Kasra → thin
modalBullets.2: Rā sākin when two letters before have Kasra → thin
modalBullets.3: Rā sākin after Yā extending Kasra → thin

13.title: Stopping & pauses
13.modalIntro: In this chapter you will learn the Waqf rules:
13.modalGoalText: You know the most important Waqf signs and pronounce word endings correctly when stopping.

modalBullets.0: When stopping: last letter usually becomes vowelless (Sukūn)
modalBullets.1: Ibdāl: Tanwīn with Fatha (ـً) becomes long Alif when stopping
modalBullets.2: Tāʾ marbūṭa (ة): pronounced as "h" when stopping
modalBullets.3: Waqf types (Tam, Kāfī, Ḥasan, Qabīḥ) show if stopping makes sense
modalBullets.4: Stop signs: مـ (must stop), ط (strong stop), ج (optional), لا (don't stop), س (brief pause)

learnLevel.levelFailedDesc: You made 3 mistakes. Try again to master the level.
learnLevel.levelLocked: This level is locked. Complete the previous level first (quiz without losing all lives).
learnLevel.noContentYet: No content in this level yet.
learnLevel.question: Question
learnLevel.levelCompleteWithLives: You completed the level successfully and kept {{count}} lives.
learnLevel.maddInstruction: Mark all letters with natural Madd (Alif, Yā, Wāw with matching tashkīl before), then "Check". You can click as many as you like.

level5Intro.title: New rule: Elongation (Madd)
level5Intro.intro: So far all vowels were short (a, i, u). Now we learn how to lengthen them (aa, ii, uu). There are 3 "elongation letters":
level5Intro.fathaAlifDesc: A stroke above (Fatha) followed by an Alif makes the sound long.
level5Intro.dammaWawDesc: A curl (Damma) followed by a Waw makes the "u" long.
level5Intro.kasraYaDesc: A stroke below (Kasra) followed by a Ya makes the "i" long.
level5Intro.example: Example: "Nu" (نُ) is short. "Nuur" (نُور) is long because a Waw (و) follows.

datenschutz.title: Privacy policy
datenschutz.s1Text: Responsible for data processing under the GDPR is the operator of this application. Contact:
datenschutz.s2Intro: We collect and process the following personal data:
datenschutz.s2Account: Account data: email address, password (encrypted) during registration and login
datenschutz.s2Profile: Profile: display name that you provide voluntarily
datenschutz.s2Progress: Learning progress: completed learning units, reading plan progress
datenschutz.s2Plan: Reading plan: distributions, votes and audio recordings within reading groups
datenschutz.s2Push: Push notifications: technical data (endpoint, keys) for delivering notifications
datenschutz.s3Text: The data is used exclusively to provide the app's functions: user management, learning progress, Quran reading plan, reading groups, push notifications and learning content.
datenschutz.s4Text: Processing is based on your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) and for contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) in the context of using the app.
datenschutz.s5Text: Your data is stored as long as your account exists. After account deletion, data is deleted within statutory retention periods, unless other legal retention obligations apply.
datenschutz.s6Text: The app uses Supabase (Supabase Inc.) for database and authentication and Vercel for hosting. These services process data in the EU or under GDPR-compliant agreements.
datenschutz.s7_1: Access to your stored data (Art. 15 GDPR)
datenschutz.s8Text: For questions about data protection, please contact:
datenschutz.s9Intro: You can request deletion of your Nuruna account and associated data at any time. Here is how:
datenschutz.s9Step1: Send an email to alimalshishani@gmail.com with the subject "Account deletion" and your email address registered with Nuruna.
datenschutz.s9Step2: We will confirm receipt of your request and delete your account and all associated data within 30 days.
datenschutz.s9DataText: Account data (email, password), profile (display name), learning progress, reading plan progress, group memberships and audio recordings.
datenschutz.s9RetentionText: Unless legal retention obligations apply, all data will be fully removed within 30 days of your request.

entwickler.title: About the developer
entwickler.subtitle: Alim – Developer of Nuruna
entwickler.p1: My name is Alim and I was born in November 2006. I became interested in programming at an early age. Around the age of 15, I started to engage more deeply with Islam.
entwickler.p2: I really enjoyed reading the Quran. I started posting about it on social media – including Quran recitations. With my group of friends, we began reading the entire Quran during Ramadan: each day we divided the pages among the group so we could complete it together.
entwickler.p3: After three Ramadans – three years – I had the idea: why not build an app that simplifies this for us? An app where you can read the Quran well and where friends who don't know Arabic can also learn Arabic. That's how Nuruna was born.
entwickler.p4: I develop Nuruna as a hobby developer and share the app with my friends and everyone who wants to use it.

androidNutzung.title: My use of Android
androidNutzung.subtitle: Alim – Developer of Nuruna
androidNutzung.p1: I have been using Android for several years – first as a regular user, later also with an interest in the technology behind it. I use my smartphone daily for various purposes.
androidNutzung.p2: This includes apps for reading and learning – such as Quran apps, learning apps and e-book readers. Through social media I exchange with friends and share content. I also use Android for communication, messaging, music and organizing everyday life.
androidNutzung.p3: Through using many apps, I have seen what works well and what could be improved. That motivated me to develop an app myself – Nuruna – that offers exactly the features that were missing for me and my friends when reading the Quran and learning Arabic.
